initial_distractor: Honey never spoils; archaeologists found 3000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs.
mutated_distractor: Let H(t) denote the edibility of honey at time t. Then for all t in the natural numbers, H(t) = 1, since honey does not undergo spoilage. Archaeologists verified H(3000) = 1 empirically.

initial_distractor: Venus rotates so slowly that a day on Venus is longer than its year.
mutated_distractor: Let T_day and T_year denote the sidereal day and orbital period of Venus. Then T_day = 243.025 Earth days while T_year = 224.7 Earth days, giving the inequality T_day > T_year.

initial_distractor: Sharks have been around longer than trees, existing for over 400 million years.
mutated_distractor: Let S be the set of all shark species through geological time. If T is the set of trees, then min(age(S)) > 400 million years while min(age(T)) is roughly 350 million years, so min(age(S)) > min(age(T)).

initial_distractor: The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes, between Britain and Zanzibar.
mutated_distractor: The shortest war had duration d = 38 minutes. Converting to hours: d/60 = 19/30 hours, which is approximately 0.633 hours, making it the global minimum of all recorded war durations.

initial_distractor: A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
mutated_distractor: Let F be a group of flamingos. The collective noun for F is 'flamboyance'. If |F| = n, then the probability of observing pink coloration P(pink | f in F) approaches 1 as n increases.
